sections.heading: Architecture Overview
sections.content:
The viz dashboard is a single-page application served by a FastAPI backend. Frontend is TypeScript compiled to ES modules (no bundler).

```
cortex/viz/
  server.py              # FastAPI app, 36 API endpoints, serves static files
  static/
    index.html           # Single HTML shell with import map + ESM script tags
    css/style.css        # CSS custom properties on :root, dark-mode only
    ts/                  # TypeScript source (14 modules)
      types.ts           # Shared interfaces: CortexNode, DocSection, Edge, etc.
      app.ts             # Orchestrator: routing, state, sidebar, init (941 lines)
      graph.ts           # Cytoscape.js graph renderer
      list.ts            # Sortable node table
      list-source-panel.ts  # Source preview panel for list view
      detail.ts          # Node detail drawer (tabs: info, source, edges, docs)
      view-utils.ts      # Shared utilities: HTML escape, Monaco loader, badges, filters
      doc-tree.ts        # DocJSON folder tree + filters
      doc-editor.ts      # Section rendering, Tiptap lifecycle, CRUD (1,430 lines)
      doc-api.ts         # Doc fetch/save/create + raw JSON
      doc-diagrams.ts    # Mermaid + Monaco diagram editor
      tests.ts           # Test explorer with JUnit
      workflow.ts        # Workflow/task step explorer
      monaco-facade.ts   # Typed wrapper for AMD Monaco loader
    js/                  # Compiled ES module output (tsc, served by FastAPI)
    tsconfig.json        # ES2022 target, strict mode
```

**Module loading:** index.html uses `<script type="importmap">` to map bare specifiers (cytoscape, mermaid) to CDN URLs. Each module uses ESM `import`/`export` — no implicit globals. Monaco remains AMD-loaded via a typed facade (`monaco-facade.ts`).

**Startup:** `cortex viz .` calls `run_server(project_root)` which calls `_apply_project()` to set module globals, `_upsert_registry()` to auto-register the project in `~/.cortex/projects.json`, then launches uvicorn. The `--no-browser` flag suppresses auto-open.

**Project switching:** The server supports hot-swapping the active project via `POST /api/projects/switch`. A `threading.Lock` (`_switch_lock`) guards the global reassignment. The frontend's `loadProject()` is re-callable — on switch it refetches `/api/meta` and `/api/all` and re-renders the full UI. Event bindings in `init()` are guarded by `_initialized` to avoid duplicate listeners.

**State management:** `App.state` is a single object holding the current view mode, all nodes/edges, staleness map, filter settings, focus mode, and dFlow integration data. All modules import from `app.ts` or receive state via function parameters.

**docs.js decomposition (Tier 3):** The former 2,505-line god object was split into 4 focused modules — `doc-tree.ts` (folder tree + filters), `doc-editor.ts` (section rendering + Tiptap lifecycle + section CRUD), `doc-diagrams.ts` (Mermaid + Monaco diagram editor), `doc-api.ts` (fetch/save/create + raw JSON). `doc-editor.ts` is the largest at 1,430 lines because section editing, tags, links, and Tiptap are tightly coupled.

sections.heading: Sequence of Operations
sections.content:
### Page Load
```
browser GET / → index.html
browser resolves import map (cytoscape, mermaid → CDN URLs)
browser loads ES modules: app.js, graph.js, list.js, detail.js, etc.
  (Monaco AMD loaded separately via monaco-facade.ts)
App.init():
  1. Wire event handlers (view buttons, filters, depth slider, focus mode)
  2. GET /api/meta → project name, node/edge/doc counts
  3. GET /api/all → full node + edge lists + staleness + verification maps
  4. Apply sidebar filters → filteredNodes
  5. Graph.render(filteredNodes, edges) → Cytoscape init
```

### Node Selection
```
User clicks node in graph or list
  App.selectNode(id):
    1. GET /api/nodes/{id} → full node detail
    2. GET /api/nodes/{id}/history → change log
    3. GET /api/nodes/{id}/tests → test results
    4. (If workflow/test) GET /api/workflow/{id}/steps or /api/test/{id}/steps
    5. Detail.show(node, history, staleness, steps, tests)
```

### Rescan
```
User clicks Rescan button
  POST /api/rescan → server runs cortex build . synchronously
  GET /api/all → refresh everything
```

sections.heading: Data Model
sections.content:
The viz server reads directly from `cortex.db`. Key queries:

| Endpoint | Tables | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `/api/all` | nodes, edges, tags, node_verification | Full graph payload. Reads `staleness` column directly — no computation (ADR-009). Config nodes (`subtype=config`) included with distinct graph coloring (`#f0883e`). |
| `/api/check` | nodes (+ filesystem via shared staleness engine) | Recomputes staleness, writes to DB, returns results (ADR-009). |
| `/api/nodes/{id}` | nodes, tags | Single node detail |
| `/api/nodes/{id}/neighborhood` | edges, nodes | Ego-network graph |
| `/api/nodes/{id}/history` | node_history | Change log |
| `/api/nodes/{id}/tests` | test_results, test_runs | Test coverage |
| `/api/workflows` | nodes (dflow_meta filter) | dFlow-decorated workflows |
| `/api/tests` | nodes (dflow_meta filter) | dFlow-decorated tests |
| `/api/fixtures` | edges (validates), nodes | Fixture→test mapping |
| `/api/junit` | test_results, test_runs | Latest JUnit results |
| `/api/docs/*` | docs, doc_sections (+ filesystem) | DocJSON CRUD |

Staleness is persisted in the `staleness` column (ADR-009). `/api/all` reads it directly (instant). `/api/check` recomputes via the shared staleness engine in `cortex/index/staleness.py` and writes back.

**Node subtype rendering:**
- `module` — blue (`#4a90d9`)
- `function` — green (`#27ae60`)
- `test` — red (`#e74c3c`)
- `docjson` — gold (`#f39c12`)
- `config` — orange (`#f0883e`) — config files from `.claude/` (settings, skills, hooks). List view renders markdown config files with inline preview.
- `external_package` — gray (`#7f8c8d`)

sections.heading: Implementation Trade-offs
sections.content:
### TypeScript ES Modules (2026-03-23)
All 7 vanilla JS files converted to 14 TypeScript modules in a big-bang cutover. `tsc` compiles to ES2022 modules — no bundler. Browser import maps resolve bare specifiers to CDN URLs. Core domain types (`CortexNode`, `DocSection`, `Edge`, `StalenessCause`) are in `types.ts` with strict interfaces. Third-party library interactions (Tiptap, Mermaid) use `any` or minimal stubs — exhaustive type coverage deferred.

### Vanilla JS, no framework (original)
The frontend uses no React/Vue/Svelte. This keeps the dependency footprint to zero for the JS side. Cytoscape.js, Monaco, and Mermaid are loaded from CDN. The trade-off is more boilerplate DOM manipulation.

### CSS custom properties (2026-03-23)
Replaced 620+ hex color literals with CSS custom properties defined on `:root`. Dead light-mode CSS rules removed (~1,100 lines). Same visual result with better maintainability for future theming.

### Full graph payload on load
`/api/all` sends every node and edge in one response. For large projects (>5000 nodes), `App.state.isLargeProject` is set and the graph view starts in list mode. This avoids slow Cytoscape rendering.

### Persisted staleness column (ADR-009)
`/api/all` now reads the `staleness` column directly from the `nodes` table — pure DB read, no file I/O. Previously, staleness was computed on-the-fly by `_compute_staleness_rich()` which re-read source files. The trade-off is staleness reflects the last build/check, not live disk state. The Check button closes this gap on demand.

**Deleted:** `_compute_staleness_from_history()` (history-table path), `_refreshStaleness` fire-and-forget JS call on page load. Both replaced by the persisted column.

### DocJSON editing in-browser
`doc-editor.ts` (1,430 lines, formerly part of docs.js 2,505 lines) implements a full inline editor: section editing, heading editing, link picker, tag management. Decomposed from the god object alongside `doc-tree.ts`, `doc-api.ts`, and `doc-diagrams.ts`.

### Node coloring by subtype (ADR-009)
Node fill colors are now keyed by `subtype` instead of `node_type`, since `document` and `constraint` node_type values are retired. Mapping: function=green, module=blue, docjson=orange, test=red, entity=purple, external_package=grey.

sections.heading: Technical Verification
sections.content:
- Graph renders with correct node colors per type and staleness borders
- Filter sidebar correctly excludes/includes node types and tags
- Focus mode shows correct ego-network at specified depth and direction
- Detail panel displays all node fields: ID, location, summary, docstring, steps, history, tests
- Rescan button triggers rebuild and refreshes all views
- DocJSON editor saves changes back to disk via PUT /api/docs/{id}
- Large project mode (>5000 nodes) defaults to list view
- Monaco source viewer loads correct file and highlights correct line
